oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Evaluation of the potential of pasteurization to reduce adenosine triphosphate luminometry counts on colostrum feeding equipment for dairy calves
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Wallace, Jodi
oai_dc:dc.dc:subject: calf
milk and colostrum feeding equipment
ATP bioluminescence
pasteurization
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: Calf feeding equipment can represent the final source of bac­terial contamination of colostrum or milk, even when visibly clean. The use of on-farm pasteurizers to reduce bacterial numbers on hard-to-clean colostrum feeding equipment such as nipples, bottles and esophageal tube feeders (ET) has not been previously evaluated. Therefore, the objective of the study was to evaluate the potential of pasteurization to reduce ATP luminometry counts on colostrum feeding equipment for dairy calves. A second objective was to evaluate if this method was comparable to a common practice of using a multi-purpose heavy-duty cleaner and degreaser (MPCD) to remove biofilm from already “cleaned” colostrum feeding equipment.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; Vol. 58 No. 2 (2025): 2025 AABP Annual Conference Proceedings; 220-221
oai_dc:dc.dc:rights: Copyright (c) 2026 American Association of Bovine Practitioners
